Title: [PDF] llvmlite Documentation
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/_/downloads/en/v0.18.0/pdf
Content: This builds the llvmlite If. Phi IRBuilder.phi(typ, name='') Create a phi node. Add incoming edges and their values using the add_incoming()

Title: [LLVMdev] phi instuction example
URL: https://groups.google.com/g/llvm-dev/c/GKp--MqLVLw
Content: lionhe...@gmail.com's profile photo. ### lionhe...@gmail.com. I'm a newbie for LLVM. What I'm trying to do is to write the simplest program containing phi IR. However, even though I wrote and if statements, what I get is LLVM IR not containing phi node. At first, I thought it is because of the optimization level but without optimization, it doesn't produce phi, and when using optimization level higher and equal to O1, the meaningless while loops are just removed, so I have a problem. Eli Bendersky's profile photo. to lionhe...@gmail.com, llv...@cs.uiuc.edu. An easy way to get a phi is to run the mem2reg pass with opt. if (a > b) return a + b + 2;. define i32 @foo(i32 %a, i32 %b) #0 {. %add = add nsw i32 %a, %b. %mul = mul nsw i32 %a, %b. %retval.0 = phi i32 [ %add1, %if.then ], [ %mul2, %if.else ]. Adam Treat's profile photo.

Title: Building and using llvmlite - a basic example - Eli Bendersky's website
URL: https://eli.thegreenplace.net/2015/building-and-using-llvmlite-a-basic-example
Content: llvmlite was created last year by the developers of Numba (a JIT compiler for scientific Python), and just recently replaced llvmpy as their bridge to LLVM. One of the reasons the Numba devs decided to ditch llvmpy in favor of a new approach is the biggest issue heavy users of LLVM as a library have - its incredible rate of API-breaking change. The LLVM C++ API is notoriously unstable and will remain this way for the foreseeable future. Since building the IR using repeated FFI calls to LLVM proved to be slow and error-prone in llvmpy, llvmlite re-implemented the IR builder in pure Python. This also reduces the "API surface" llvmlite uses, since the textual representation of LLVM IR is one of the more stable things in LLVM. LLVM has a great tool named llvm-config in its binary image, and the Makefile in llvmlite uses it, which means building llvmlite with any version of LLVM I want is just a simple matter of running:.

Title: 3.4. IR builders — llvmlite 0.5.0 documentation
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/en/v0.9.0/ir/builder.html
Content: `IRBuilder` is the workhorse of LLVM IR generation. A `IRBuilder` internally maintains a current basic block, and a pointer inside the block’s list of instructions. When adding a new instruction, it is inserted at that point and the pointer is then advanced after the new instruction. A `IRBuilder` also maintains a reference to metadata describing the current source location, which will be attached to all inserted instructions. If *block* (a `Block`) is given, the builder starts right at the end of this basic block. :   The basic block the builder is operating on. :   The function the builder is operating on. :   Append a basic block, with the given optional *name*, to the current function. The current block is also changed to the instruction’s basic block. :   A context manager which positions the builder either at the end of the basic *block*, if it is not terminated, or just before the *block*‘s terminator:. `IRBuilder.``add`(*lhs*, *rhs*, *name=''*, *flags=()*)¶.

Title: What exactly PHI instruction does and how to use it in LLVM
URL: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11485531/what-exactly-phi-instruction-does-and-how-to-use-it-in-llvm
Content: ### your communities. ### more stack exchange communities. Communities for your favorite technologies. Stack Overflow for Teams is now called **Stack Internal**. # What exactly PHI instruction does and how to use it in LLVM. *The 'phi' instruction is used to implement the φ node in the SSA graph representing the function.*. Could someone explain what is a Phi node, and how to implement `||` with it? A phi node is an instruction used to select a value depending on the predecessor of the current block (Look here to see the full hierarchy - it's also used as a value, which is one of the classes which it inherits from). Phi nodes are necessary due to the structure of the SSA (static single assignment) style of the LLVM code - for example, the following C++ function. %r.addr = alloca i8, align 1. Otherwise, it is equal to the value of `%tobool2` - and that's exactly what we get from the following IR line:.
